title: Jain Temple, Kidanganad
tagline: Jain Temple, Kidanganad — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Sultan Bathery temple, originally known as Bathery Jain Temple, is a famous Jain temple located at Sultan Bathery, earlier known as Kidanganad town of Kerala.
overview.description: Sultan Bathery temple, originally known as Bathery Jain Temple, is a famous Jain temple located at Sultan Bathery, earlier known as Kidanganad town of Kerala.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Syro-Malankara Catholic Eparchy of Bathery
topPlaces.description: The Syro-Malankara Catholic Eparchy of Bathery is an eparchy of the Syro-Malankara Catholic Church in Sulthan Bathery, in Kerala, India. As of 2024 Aboon Joseph Mar Thomas was the bishop.

topPlaces.name: Kuppadi
topPlaces.description: Kuppadi is a village near Sultan Bathery in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.The famous St Mary's College, Sulthan Bathery is situated in this village.

topPlaces.name: Nenmeni
topPlaces.description: Nenmeni is a village near Bathery in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Sultan Bathery
topPlaces.description: Sulthan Bathery is a major municipal town in the Wayanad district of Kerala, India. It serves as the administrative headquarters of Sulthan Bathery taluk and is the most commercially active urban center in the district.

topPlaces.name: Valavayal
topPlaces.description: Valavayal is a small town in Wayanad district of Kerala, the people primarily depend on agriculture for a living. The town has the Meenangadi-Pulpally road passing through it.

topPlaces.name: Edakkal caves
topPlaces.description: The Edakkal caves are two natural caves at a remote location in Sultan Bathery in the Wayanad district of Kerala in India. They lie 1,200 m (3,900 ft) above sea level on Ambukutty Mala, near an ancient trade route connecting the high mountains of Mysore to the ports of the Malabar Coast.

topPlaces.name: Phantom Rock
topPlaces.description: Phantom Rock is a tourist destination in Ambalavayal in the district of Wayanad in Kerala. It is a natural metamorphic rock formation resembling a human skull, and thus is known as Phantom Rock.

topPlaces.name: Edakkal
topPlaces.description: Edakkal is a remote location in Wayanad district of Kerala, India, the site of the Edakkal Caves. The tallest mountain in the district, Ambukuthi mala, is located here.

items.note: The Syro-Malankara Catholic Eparchy of Bathery is an eparchy of the Syro-Malankara Catholic Church in Sulthan Bathery, in Kerala, India. As of 2024 Aboon Joseph Mar Thomas was the bishop.
Kuppadi is a village near Sultan Bathery in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.The famous St Mary's College, Sulthan Bathery is situated in this village.
Nenmeni is a village near Bathery in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.

Sulthan Bathery is a major municipal town in the Wayanad district of Kerala, India. It serves as the administrative headquarters of Sulthan Bathery taluk and is the most commercially active urban center in the district.
Valavayal is a small town in Wayanad district of Kerala, the people primarily depend on agriculture for a living. The town has the Meenangadi-Pulpally road passing through it.

The Edakkal caves are two natural caves at a remote location in Sultan Bathery in the Wayanad district of Kerala in India. They lie 1,200 m (3,900 ft) above sea level on Ambukutty Mala, near an ancient trade route connecting the high mountains of Mysore to the ports of the Malabar Coast.
Phantom Rock is a tourist destination in Ambalavayal in the district of Wayanad in Kerala. It is a natural metamorphic rock formation resembling a human skull, and thus is known as Phantom Rock.
Edakkal is a remote location in Wayanad district of Kerala, India, the site of the Edakkal Caves. The tallest mountain in the district, Ambukuthi mala, is located here.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jain Temple, Kidanganad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jain Temple, Kidanganad is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jain Temple, Kidanganad?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (67 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (84 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jain Temple, Kidanganad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jain Temple, Kidanganad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jain Temple, Kidanganad?
faq.a: Stays in Jain Temple, Kidanganad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jain Temple, Kidanganad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Syro-Malankara Catholic Eparchy of Bathery, Kuppadi, Nenmeni, Sultan Bathery, Valavayal.
